mnemonics: Naive Bayes for Continuous Attributes: Estimate Probabilities from Independent Features
Bayes' Theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)
keyPoints: Definition: Naïve Bayes for Continuous Attributes is an extension of Naïve Bayes for Categorical Attributes, where continuous attributes are handled using probability distributions (e.g. Gaussian, Uniform)
Assumptions: Features are conditionally independent (i.e. P(X|Y) = P(X)), and each feature has a specific probability distribution (e.g. normal, uniform)
Implementation: Use a Gaussian distribution for continuous features, and compute the probability of the feature using its mean, variance, and standard deviation
